story.iWant: realistic test data generate edebilmek için factory pattern
story.soThat: Faker.js ile Turkish test data kolayca oluşturabileyim
story.tasks: Task 1: Install Faker.js v8+ and create factories directory structure Task 2: Implement UserFactory class with Faker integration Task 3: Implement remaining factory classes (Role, Permission, File) Task 4: Test factory implementations Task 5: Integration with existing seeder classes

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. prisma/factories/ directory oluşturulmuş 2. Factory classes: user.factory.ts, role.factory.ts, permission.factory.ts, file.factory.ts 3. Static methods: generate() (single), generateMany(count) (bulk) 4. Faker.js v8+ integration: Turkish locale for realistic data 5. Type safety: Prisma types kullanarak generic factory 6. Override support: factory(overrides) → merge defaults + overrides 7. Realistic data: Turkish names, emails, phone numbers (05xx), addresses

doc.title: Epic 13: Advanced Seeder Infrastructure
doc.section: Story 13.2: Model Factory Pattern & Faker Integration
doc.snippet: Story 13.2: Model Factory Pattern & Faker Integration. As a developer, I want realistic test data generate edebilmek için factory pattern, So that Faker.js ile Turkish test data kolayca oluşturabileyim.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Advanced Seeder Infrastructure
doc.snippet: prisma/factories/ directory with user.factory.ts, role.factory.ts, file.factory.ts. Individual factory classes with static seed() method pattern.

doc.title: Story 13.1: Prisma Native Seeding Entry Point
doc.snippet: Modular seeder pattern: Each entity has its own seeder class with static seed() method. Main entry point at prisma/seed.ts implementing Prisma's native seeding feature.

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert. Test coverage requirements: Utilities 100%, Services 80%+, Controllers 70%+, Repositories 70%+.

file.reason: Existing user seeder that will integrate with UserFactory for bulk user creation
Role seeder with static seed() method pattern to follow for RoleFactory implementation
Permission seeder to integrate with PermissionFactory for generating permission data
File seeder to integrate with FileFactory for generating sample file metadata
Main seeding entry point that orchestrates all seeder classes in dependency order

npm.package: Password hashing utility for test users (Story 13.6)
TypeScript execution for seeders and factories
Prisma ORM with native seeding support

story-context.constraints: - Static factory methods with Prisma type definitions for type safety - Use Prisma.{Entity}CreateInput types to ensure factory output matches Prisma schema - Override support via Partial merge pattern for flexible data generation - Turkish locale integration for realistic data (names, phone 05xx format) - Idempotent operations: Generated data must be compatible with upsert() and createMany(skipDuplicates: true) - Import organization following Epic 12.5 8-category pattern - TypeScript strict mode compliance (Epic 12.4) - No breaking changes to existing seeders (Story 13.1) - Environment-based seeding compatible (Story 13.5)

interface.name: UserFactory.generateMany()
interface.signature: static generateMany(count: number, overrides: Partial<Prisma.UserCreateInput> = {}): Prisma.UserCreateInput[]

tests.standards: Factory tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. UserFactory and other factories should have comprehensive unit tests covering generate() with various overrides, generateMany() for bulk generation, TypeScript type safety, Turkish locale data validation (05xx phone format, Turkish names), and override merging logic. Tests should verify idempotency with seeder operations.
tests.locations: test/factories/ directory for factory unit tests, prisma/factories/ for implementation, test/seeders/ for integration tests
tests.ideas: AC 1-2: Verify prisma/factories/ directory created with 4 factory files AC 3: Test generate() produces single record, generateMany(count) produces array of correct length AC 4: Validate Turkish phone format (05## ### ## ##), Turkish names via faker.locale = 'tr' AC 5: TypeScript type safety test - ensure return types match Prisma schema AC 6: Override test - verify defaults merged with overrides correctly AC 7: Data realism test - validate emails are valid format, names are Turkish, dates are reasonable
